page: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing 2 threads per core. Memory capacity is 128.0 GB, which translates to a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities. The server achieves top-tier status in PassMark CPU score, single-threaded gzip compression, and static web serving throughput. Multi-core stress-ng performance, PassMark database operations, and Redis SET operations fall into the strong category. For artificial intelligence workloads, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions enable strong prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large language models, though text generation speeds remain in the average tier.

The primary tradeoff of the D64ls_v7 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local storage, requiring external premium storage attachments. This configuration yields high compute density and cost efficiency for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is suited for compute-heavy tasks, web serving, and parallel batch processing rather than memory-intensive databases.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, representing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, and contains no local storage or GPU resources. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance leverages advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in PassMark CPU tests, static web serving, and single-threaded compression, alongside strong multi-core processing and LLM prompt processing. The lack of local storage and low memory density represent key architectural tradeoffs, positioning this server as a cost-efficient option for compute-bound workloads, web serving, and parallel batch processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier CPU performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier CPU and web serving performance.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated Intel Xeon cores, low memory allocation, and top-tier processing performance for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier performance in PassMark CPU benchmarks and static web serving
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
No bundled local storage, requiring external premium storage solutions
Best suited for compute-intensive tasks, web serving, and batch processing
